scope.summary: C6 isomorphic split of dispatch/mod.rs: the C2 orientation + fleet-orientation tool family extracted behind a facade seam into dispatch/orient.rs, with the dispatch/mod.rs file-size ratchet lowered in a paired commit; no tool-surface or behavior change.
out_of_scope: Splitting the other dispatch/mod.rs tool families (this bead extracts one seam at a time; the remaining families stay for follow-up C6 beads)
The concurrent dispatch/tests.rs ratchet violation (another pane's A1e RLS/VPD test additions; rule 13, not this bead's file)
Any change to the classifier fuzz target or the registry tool surface

entry_points.name: orient/fleet-orient seam extraction (commit 82ed7b99)
entry_points.trace: Extracted 453 lines (8 structs + 1 enum + 10 free fns) from dispatch/mod.rs into dispatch/orient.rs, wired via 'mod orient; use orient::*;' (the established facade pattern matching limits.rs/args.rs). All items pub(super); effective visibility within dispatch unchanged. git diff --stat: 491 insertions / 466 deletions across mod.rs + orient.rs.

entry_points.name: isomorphism — tool surface unchanged
entry_points.trace: registry.rs still exactly 34 oracle_ tools + 25 compat aliases (verified by parsing TOOL_NAMES). Classifier fuzz target untouched (lives in oraclemcp-guard). cargo clippy -p oraclemcp --lib -- -D warnings: clean.

entry_points.name: isomorphism — orient/fleet contract tests
entry_points.trace: cargo test -p oraclemcp --lib -- orient fleet_orient fleet_catalog: 5 passed, 0 failed (fleet_orient_compares_each_reachable_profile_to_a_stable_baseline, fleet_catalog_masks_each_profile_before_merging_results, fleet_orient_returns_every_visible_profile_when_one_lane_is_unreachable, fleet_catalog_hidden_profile_is_indistinguishable_from_absence, orient_assembles_selector_stable_snapshot_and_reloads_on_catalog_revision).

entry_points.name: file-size ratchet lowered (commit e61a2b19)
entry_points.trace: scripts/oraclemcp_arch_fitness_lint.sh dispatch/mod.rs ratchet lowered 15623 -> 15167 to match the post-split size (wc -l = 15167). Landed as an immediate follow-up commit because the split commit is already on main in the shared checkout.

limitations: This bead extracts ONE tool-family seam; dispatch/mod.rs still holds the remaining families for follow-up C6 beads.
The arch-fitness lint currently reports a dispatch/tests.rs ratchet violation (15703 > 15676) caused by another pane's concurrent A1e RLS/VPD test additions — out of scope for this bead (rule 13).
65 dispatch tests fail in the shared tree from the concurrent A1e RLS/VPD semantic-purity work (ForbiddenStatement/UnprovenSideEffect), NOT from this extraction; all orient/fleet tests pass.
follow_ups: Continue C6 de-monolith of dispatch/mod.rs one tool-family seam at a time (remaining families), each lowering the ratchet in its split commit.
